title: paepalanthus hymenolepis
intro: Paepalanthus hymenolepis, also known as Pilocarpus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to stimulate the contraction of the iris, thereby reducing intraocular pressure, and is used in the treatment of glaucoma. The plant is rich in pilocarpine, which has been found to have health benefits such as reducing blood pressure and improving circulation. Herbal preparations of Paepalanthus hymenolepis include tinctures and infusions, which can be used to treat various conditions, including hypertension and conjunctivitis.
